title: mysql2 createConnection with ssl.rejectUnauthorized: false — the MySQL TLS certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: The mysql2 connection validates the server certificate (ssl.rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the database connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: createConnection is configured with ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false }, disabling validation of the MySQL server's TLS certificate. Anyone on the network path can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the database connection — every query and credential is exposed. Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; provide the provider's CA via ssl.ca.
params.absentCallDetail: No createConnection call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: createConnection does not set ssl.rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false } is the mysql2 equivalent of the node-postgres footgun, pasted to connect to a managed MySQL (PlanetScale/RDS) without wiring the CA. Nothing errors, so it ships and disables MITM protection on the database.
